channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
item.description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

item.title: Honda’s hybrid future starts with new Accord and RDX prototypes
item.description: Honda revealed prototypes of two new hybrid models, an Accord sedan and the Acura RDX SUV, during its annual business briefing this week, built on a platform that it says will begin launching next year. The RDX was announced earlier this year as Honda's first SUV to feature the next-gen version of its two-motor hybrid […]

item.title: Developers can now debug and evaluate AI agents locally with Raindrop's open source tool Workshop
item.description: Observability startup Raindrop AI’s new open source, MIT Licensed "Workshop" tool, launched today, gives developers something that they've likely wanted, perhaps subconsciously, since the agentic AI era kicked off in earnest last year: a local debugger and evaluation tool specifically designed for AI agents, allowing devs to see all the traces of what their agent has been doing in a single, lightweight Structured Query Language (SQL) database file (.db)It functions as a local daemon and UI that streams every token, tool call, and decision to a local dashboard—typically hosted at localhost:5899...

item.title: Closing time
item.description: Today was closing arguments in the Musk v. Altman trial, and I almost feel bad writing about the unbelievable demolition derby I just witnessed. Steven Molo, Musk's lawyer, stumbled over his words. He at one point called Greg Brockman - a co-defendant - Greg Altman. He erroneously claimed that Musk wasn't asking for money and […]

item.title: New 3D memory architecture revives old camera technology to smash through AI memory wall - NAND + DRAM hybrid promises to make memory cheaper, faster and with 'unlimited endurance'
item.description: Old camera tech has been employed to form a 3D hybrid of NAND and DRAM tech using IGZO instead of silicon.

item.title: Apple ends education discount loophole for non-students and non-teachers
item.description: Apple is now verifying that consumers are actually a student or teacher before they can make a discounted purchase via its Education Store.

item.title: DJI just unveiled the dual-lens Osmo Pocket 4P cinematic camera at Cannes
item.description: At Cannes, the Chinese company pulled back the curtain on its latest gimbal camera, the Osmo Pocket 4P.

item.title: 'Not in my backyard': New Gallup data shows most Americans would rather have a nuclear power plant in their neighborhood than a data center
item.description: Gallup data illustrates rising opposition to data centers across the US, with barely anyone in favor of new facilities.

item.title: Forget the App Store — Apple could be about to launch the ‘agent store’ for AI apps, and it could transform how you use your iPhone
item.description: A report claims Apple is working to welcome AI agents to its App Store as it attempts to embrace the future.

item.title: Graduation ceremony speaker blasted by boos when she calls AI 'the next industrial revolution' — why Gen Z cheers for a time before AI
item.description: A commencement speaker was booed for calling AI an industrial revolution. Why does Gen Z hate AI so much?

item.title: These habits undermine your authority at work
item.description: You don’t have to be the loudest person in the room to command it, but these three habits might be quietly costing you credibility without you even realizing it. From the words you choose to how you walk through the door, here’s what to change so your ideas actually land. You don’t have to be the loudest person in the room to command it, but these three habits might be quietly costing you credibility without you even realizing it. From the words you choose to how you walk through the door, here’s what to change so your ideas actually land....

item.title: Xbox Elite Controller 3 leaked by Brazilian regulator
item.description: It's a tough day to work at Microsoft PR.

item.title: He says Kim Kardashian ruined his life with one Instagram post. Now he owes her six figures
item.description: After suing Kardashian over a mistaken identity post, a New York man has been ordered to cover a large chunk of her legal fees. A case of mistaken identity can cost you, especially if it involves Kim Kardashian.

item.title: The original Doom soundtrack is officially in the Library of Congress
item.description: Other new inductees include Beyonce's 'Single Ladies' and Taylor Swift's '1989.'

item.title: Metroid Prime 4: Beyond got its first big discount
item.description: The most graphically-impressive first-person shooter made for the Nintendo Switch is $20 off at Best Buy. Right now, you can buy the physical version of Metroid Prime 4: Beyond for $39.99 ($20 off). The game looks surprisingly good on the 2017 hardware, but runs way better on the Switch 2 with the additional $10 upgrade […]

item.title: Cerebras stock nearly doubles on day one as AI chipmaker hits $100 billion — what it means for AI infrastructure
item.description: Cerebras Systems, the Silicon Valley chipmaker that built the world's largest commercial AI processor, erupted onto the Nasdaq on Wednesday, opening at $350 per share — nearly double its $185 IPO price — and rocketing past a $100 billion market capitalization in its first hours of trading. The debut instantly crowned Cerebras as one of the most valuable semiconductor companies on Earth and validated a decade-long bet that the AI industry would eventually demand a fundamentally different kind of chip.The company sold 30 million shares at $185 apiece, raising $5.55 billion in what Bloomberg repo...

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
item.description: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.

item.title: Brick quietly dropped one of their best features yet — now you can lock your phone from anywhere
item.description: Brick quietly changed how you can lock your phone. Now, you can hold a button within the app and Brick it remotely.

item.title: Razer updates the Blade 18 with new chips, a specced-out model costs $7,000
item.description: The 2026 model adds the Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus processor.

item.title: Utah just approved a data center twice the size of Manhattan that will consume more electricity than the entire state
item.description: A data center twice the size of Manhattan that will consume around 9GW of power has just been approved in Utah.

item.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
item.description: Meta is rolling out new features to its Meta Ray-Ban Display smart glasses, including bringing the ability to write messages just with hand gestures to all users. You'll be able to use the feature in WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram and with "native Android and iOS messaging," Meta says. The feature, which relies on the Meta Ray-Ban […]

item.title: We Now Know How Many People the CDC Is Monitoring for Hantavirus
item.description: There are no confirmed cases in the US, but 41 people who were potentially exposed to the Andes virus are in quarantine or being monitored for symptoms.
